ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via card name, FFTCG category 'Theatrhythm,' and canonical Chocobo visual identifiers: yellow feathers, orange crest, round blue eye, plump galliform silhouette. IP is Square Enix-internal (Final Fantasy franchise mascot). No external IP risk.

trivia_bullets.text: The Chocobo is one of the Final Fantasy franchise's defining mascots, debuting in Final Fantasy II (1988) and appearing in virtually every mainline FF title since. Designed by Koichi Ishii; the name derives from the Morinaga 'Choco Ball' confection. The Chocobo Theme (Nobuo Uematsu) is one of the most remixed tracks in video game history.

Opus VI was released July 13, 2018 (130 cards: 54 commons, 36 rares, 26 heroes, 14 legends). This card's category is Theatrhythm — the art draws from Theatrhythm Final Fantasy (Nintendo 3DS, 2012), a rhythm game released to celebrate the franchise's 25th anniversary. In Theatrhythm Field Music Sequences, a Chocobo appears when players execute a successful Feature Drive.
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki: Theatrhythm Final Fantasy

trivia_bullets.text: Forward type, Wind element, Cost 3, Power 3000, Standard Unit job, Category: Theatrhythm. When Chocobo enters the field, you may play 1 card named Chocobo from your hand onto the field. Self-replicating entry effect — playing one Chocobo immediately chains into a second, creating a two-Forward field state for the cost of 3 Wind. Common rarity.

The Theatrhythm visual register — chibi proportions, musical-note background, soft palette — is distinct from standard FFTCG Chocobo art. Opus VI deliberately drew from the Theatrhythm game's visual language for this category subset, making this card visually legible as a crossover artifact within FFTCG rather than a generic franchise-mascot depiction.
sources: WebSearch: Opus VI Collection Final Fantasy Card Game Wikia
